name: Milky Way
description: The Milky Way is the galaxy that contains our Solar System and is home to billions of stars, planets, and nebulae.

name: Andromeda
description: The Andromeda Galaxy is the nearest major galaxy to the Milky Way and is expected to collide with it in about 4.5 billion years.

name: Whirlpool Galaxy
description: The Whirlpool Galaxy is famous for its beautiful spiral structure and active star formation regions.

name: Sombrero Galaxy
description: The Sombrero Galaxy is known for its bright central bulge and prominent dust lane, giving it the appearance of a sombrero hat.

name: Triangulum Galaxy
description: The Triangulum Galaxy is the third-largest member of the Local Group after the Milky Way and Andromeda.

name: Messier 87
description: Messier 87 contains one of the largest known supermassive black holes and was the first black hole ever photographed.

name: Large Magellanic Cloud
description: The Large Magellanic Cloud is a satellite galaxy of the Milky Way and contains active star-forming regions.

name: Small Magellanic Cloud
description: The Small Magellanic Cloud is a nearby dwarf galaxy that interacts gravitationally with both the Milky Way and the Large Magellanic Cloud.
